justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified — output calls it 'tight coupling where none previously existed' between two bounded contexts. (2) Data ownership raised — StockLevel owned by Context A, 'available stock' semantics questioned throughout. (3) TOCTOU/race condition identified — AC-4 tests simultaneous orders, BR-3 explains the concurrent check problem, edge case explicitly covers it. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — 'Stock Reservation' called out as a 'not-yet-defined concept' and 'missing domain concept' absent from both contexts. (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural pattern — three escalations covering blocking semantics, indicator freshness, and integration pattern (sync vs event-driven), all recommending architectural decisions. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 and BR-3 define the new invariants with testable assertions.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough TOCTOU analysis — not just mentioned but modeled with BR-3's reservation-aware check and AC-4's concurrent order test case
Stock Reservation explicitly called out as a missing first-class domain concept that exists in neither context currently
Three well-structured escalations with concrete options and recommendations, covering blocking semantics, indicator freshness, and integration pattern
Edge cases cover reservation release on cancellation, phantom reservations, and the advisory-vs-authoritative distinction for the stock indicator
AC-6 explicitly flags the atomic-vs-partial order blocking as undefined rather than silently resolving it
Confidence level appropriately low (45%) given the ambiguity, with clear separation of high and low confidence areas
